title: Elaeocarpus zollingeri
taxonomy: Elaeocarpus zollingeri is classified within the family Elaeocarpaceae. This family is a part of the order Oxalidales, which is a grouping of flowering plant families. The order Oxalidales is further classified under the subclass Rosidae and the class Magnoliopsida. Elaeocarpus zollingeri is a species of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia, and its classification reflects its botanical relationships with other plants in the region. Its classification also highlights its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Elaeocarpaceae family.
uses: Elaeocarpus zollingeri, a plant native to Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. The plant's leaves and bark are also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Elaeocarpus zollingeri's medicinal uses.

intro: Elaeocarpus zollingeri, also known as the Zollinger's Elaeocarpus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae, native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Elaeocarpus zollingeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Elaeocarpus zollingeri is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
